title: syzygium paniculatum
intro: Syzygium paniculatum, also known as the Australian waterberry or lilly pilly, is a shrub or small tree native to eastern Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Syzygium paniculatum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and its berries can be eaten fresh or used to make jams and preserves.
